method: Normalized prefix matching of all 139 unmapped features' web_feature and webdx_usecounter_enum fields against all ChromeStatus counter names (CSS, WebDX, legacy HTML/JS). Multiple normalization strategies applied: lowercase, strip hyphens/underscores/dots, then exact and prefix matching.

result.conclusion: Mapping was already exhaustive. Zero valid new mappings found.

candidates.rationale: IntlLocaleInfo is a different API (Intl.Locale.info), not Intl.Locale.variants. Counter measures .info property usage.
FileError is a legacy error interface, not the File API or Blob.bytes().
'system' is the CSS system font keyword, not system colors.

Counter measures private DNS alias usage in local network access — a specific sub-behavior, not overall LNA feature adoption. Low confidence; not equivalent to feature adoption. Not added per no-ambiguous-mappings rule.
Counter measures getComputedStyle called outside flat tree — a misuse pattern, not custom property enumeration.

candidates.feature: Remove explicit border color UA rule
candidates.rationale: table-layout is a different CSS property. Feature is about UA stylesheet changes.
'Scope' is the CSS :scope concept, not scoped custom element registries.

candidates.feature: Preserving dropEffect from dragover to drop
candidates.rationale: 'd' is the CSS path() shape property. Normalization artifact: 'draganddrop' starts with 'd'.
IntlSegmenter is a specific Intl API, not the ICU version update.
pointer-events is the CSS property. Feature is about the JS pointer events API.
animation is the CSS shorthand property. Feature is about the JS event handler.
Counter measures LCP explicitly requested via renderTime — a specific sub-behavior. Feature changes candidate emission logic. Not equivalent. Not added per no-ambiguous-mappings rule.
Wrong web_feature assignment. Overscroll gestures unrelated to CSS animation property.
Same as 5140028068069376: 'd' is CSS path shape, not drag-and-drop.

candidates.feature: Pointer event suppression on drag start
candidates.rationale: Same as 5140028068069376: 'd' is CSS path shape, not drag-and-drop.

canonicalBreakdown.method: Exclusive priority categories: web_feature (non-null, non-'Missing feature') > webdx_usecounter_enum (only) > origin_trial_id (only) > neither

priorCheckpoint.method: Non-exclusive categories; 'Missing feature' counted as web_feature; features with both wf+wdx counted in both

canonical.method: Exclusive priority: wf > wdx > ot > neither; 'Missing feature' excluded from web_feature

deltas.web_feature: -21 (excluded 'Missing feature' = 24 entries, but 3 had OT IDs reclassified)
deltas.webdx_enum: -4 (features with both wf+wdx now classified as web_feature only)
deltas.neither: +23 (includes 'Missing feature' entries + features with only OT IDs reclassified)

breakdownReconciliation.canonicalDenominator: The canonical breakdown uses exclusive priority categories. 139 total is consistent across both methods — only the category assignment differs.
